content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
75	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
76	
77	### Prover Agent
78	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
79	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
80	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
81	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
82	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
83	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
84	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
85	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
86	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
87	
88	### Refactor Agent
89	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
90	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
91	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
92	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
93	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
94	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
95	
96	### Review Agent
97	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
98	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
99	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
100	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
101	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
102	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 011 — `genus` closure scheduled)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor adds the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body can use the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body itself is filled by the
21	iter-011 prover round with the probe-confirmed one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-
40	-- Sketch of the route once Phase A is available:
41	--
42	-- noncomputable def OXAsAddCommGrpSheaf
43	--     {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) :
44	--     CategoryTheory.Sheaf
45	--       (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology
46	--         C.left.toPresheafedSpace.carrier) AddCommGrpCat :=
47	--   ⟨C.left.sheaf.1.comp
48	--     (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat), by
49	--     -- Use `Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp` plus the fact that
50	--     -- forgetful functors `CommRingCat ⥤ RingCat ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`
51	--     -- create limits of multispan diagrams.
52	--     sorry⟩
53	--
54	-- noncomputable def H1OC
55	--     {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) : Type _ :=
56	--   (OXAsAddCommGrpSheaf C).H 1
57	--
58	-- The `Module k`-instance on `H1OC` would come from the `k`-algebra
59	-- structure on `Γ(C, O_C)` (via `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (.of k)`),
60	-- propagated through the abelian-group cohomology by functoriality.
61	-/
62	
63	-- data
64	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
65	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
66	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
67	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
68	  -- Iter-011 closure scaffold: the body is filled by the prover round with
69	  -- `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)`. The
70	  -- `noncomputable` modifier is authorised by the user (recorded
71	  -- `2026-05-07` in `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`) because the honest body uses
72	  -- `Module.finrank`, `Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and `toModuleKSheaf` — all
73	  -- noncomputable Mathlib API. Argument types, names, and order remain
74	  -- verbatim per `archon-protected.yaml`.
75	  sorry
76	
77	end AlgebraicGeometry
78

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	prover  (iteration 011 — first protected sorry closure: `genus`. Iter-011 refactor has executed and verified; `Genus.lean` carries the `noncomputable` modifier and the `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK` import; sorry count unchanged at 10. Iter-011 prover round will fill the body of `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` with the probe-confirmed one-liner `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)`. Sorry count expected to drop 10 → 9 (8 protected + 1 deferred `representable`). **First protected sorry to close.**)
5	
6	## Stages
7	- [x] init
8	- [x] autoformalize
9	- [ ] prover
10	- [ ] polish
11	
12	## Big-picture context
13	
14	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
15	
16	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). Iter-005/006/007 closed Phase A steps 1–5 + polish. Iter-008 closed Track 2 (étale sheafification of `PicardFunctorAb`). Iter-009 closed the Phase A step 5/6 bridge (`HModule` infrastructure for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology). Iter-010 closed the Phase A step 6 *algebraic preliminary* (`HModule_zero_linearEquiv`). **Iter-011 closes the protected `genus` sorry — the first of the 9 deliverables.**
17	
18	## Iteration 010 prover-round verification (prior pass)
19	
20	The iter-010 prover round closed `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv` with the probe-confirmed body `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`. Plan-agent verification (the iter-011 first pass) confirmed sorry count `11 → 10`, kernel-only axioms, and 100 % first-edit closure; the iter-010 prover round was therefore accepted. See `task_done.md` and `STRATEGY.md` for the full record.
21	
22	## Iteration 011 refactor verification (this post-refactor pass)
23	
24	The iter-011 refactor agent has executed `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` (now cleared). Plan-agent post-refactor verification (this pass) confirms:
25	
26	- **File touched (single)**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean`. All other files untouched.
27	- **Change 1 — import added**: line 7 reads `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK` (added immediately after the existing `import Mathlib`). This brings `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule` and `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` into scope.
28	- **Change 2 — `noncomputable` modifier added on `def genus`**: line 65 now reads `noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))` (compare iter-010 `def genus`). Argument types, names, order, and return type `: ℕ` are preserved verbatim. The body remains `sorry` (the prover round fills it).
29	- **Change 3 — file docstring rewritten**: the iter-001 status block has been replaced with the iter-011 closure-scaffold docstring (lines 9–29). No semantic content — just keeps the file's prose aligned with the iter-011 closure plan.
30	- **Change 4 — body comment block rewritten**: the iter-001 commentary on the `sorry` body has been replaced with iter-011 commentary referencing the user's `noncomputable` authorisation. Cosmetic.
31	- **archon-protected.yaml**: unchanged. `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean: AlgebraicGeometry.genus` is still the single entry on line 5–6. The `noncomputable` modifier is the only modifier-level change; argument types, names, and order are not touched. **The protected signature is preserved.**
32	- **Sorry count**: 10 (unchanged from pre-refactor baseline). `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary` returns `10 total across 4 file(s)`: 5 `Jacobian.lean`, 3 `AbelJacobi.lean`, 1 `Genus.lean`, 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`. Matches the directive's expected post-refactor count exactly.
33	- **Compilation**: `lean_diagnostic_messages` on `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` returns
34	  ```json
35	  {"success":true,"items":[{"severity":"warning","message":"declaration uses `sorry`","line":65,"column":19}],"failed_dependencies":[]}
36	  ```
37	  Exactly one warning — the expected `declaration uses 'sorry'` at the body of `genus`. Zero errors.
38	- **Body probe (post-refactor sanity check, this pass)**: plan-agent `lean_run_code` on
39	  ```lean
40	  noncomputable example {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	      [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	      [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	  ```
45	  returns `{"success":true,"diagnostics":[]}` against the post-refactor `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK` + `open CategoryTheory Limits` + `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`. The body is drop-in for the prover round at universe `u`.
46	- **Negative-control probe**: the same body without the `noncomputable` modifier fails with `failed to compile definition, consider marking it as 'noncomputable' because it depends on 'Module.finrank', which is 'noncomputable'`. The `noncomputable` modifier is therefore **necessary** (the refactor was not gratuitous).
47	
48	The iter-011 refactor is therefore accepted. The prover round can proceed.
49	
50	## Current Objectives
51	
52	The dispatcher fans out one prover per `.lean` file under `## Current Objectives`. There is a single objective this round.
53	
54	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` — fill the body of the protected `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`
55	
56	**Goal.** Replace the `sorry` body of `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` (line 75 of `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean`, between the `noncomputable def genus … : ℕ :=` signature on lines 65–67 and the `end AlgebraicGeometry` on line 77) with the **probe-confirmed one-liner**
57	
58	```lean
59	Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
60	```
61	
62	This is the honest mathematical definition `g(C) = dim_k H¹(C, O_C)`: the structure sheaf `O_C` is realised as the iter-006 `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C : Sheaf (Opens.gT C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat k)`; the iter-009 `Scheme.HModule k F 1` returns `H¹` as an object of the underlying type with `Module k`-instance via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` (auto-resolved by typeclass through the `noncomputable abbrev` reducibility of `HModule`); `Module.finrank k …` returns the `k`-dimension as a `ℕ` (zero if infinite-dimensional, the dimension if finite).
63	
64	**Body to use (verbatim — drop `sorry` and place this in its stead):**
65	
66	```lean
67	Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
68	```
69	
70	The body is a single application; no helper lemmas, no `by` block, no tactic mode. The `noncomputable` modifier (already added by the iter-011 refactor) is what allows this body to typecheck.
71	
72	**Probe evidence.** Plan-agent `lean_run_code` (this pass, post-refactor) confirms the body typechecks end-to-end at universe `u` against the post-refactor file imports (`import Mathlib` + `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`) plus the file's `open CategoryTheory Limits` + `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`. No name-resolution adjustment is forced. Specifically:
73	
74	- `Scheme.HModule` resolves under `namespace AlgebraicGeometry` to `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule` (defined in `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L185, iter-009).
75	- `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` resolves similarly to `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (`Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L152, iter-006).
76	- `Module.finrank` is the standard Mathlib `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Finrank.lean` definition; it returns `ℕ` for any `Module k M` (zero on infinite-dimensional `M`).
77	- The `Module k`-instance on `Scheme.HModule k F 1` is auto-resolved through the `noncomputable abbrev` reducibility of `HModule` to `Abelian.Ext _ F 1`, plus the `Linear k` enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`, plus `Abelian.Ext.instModule`.
78	
79	**Blueprint.** `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` already contains the iter-011 informal proof sketch in the `\begin{proof} ... \end{proof}` block of `def:genus`. The prover does not need to update the blueprint; the review agent will add the `\leanok` markers post-prover-round.
80	
81	**`archon-protected.yaml`.** Untouched by the prover round. The single entry `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean: AlgebraicGeometry.genus` remains. The prover may not edit `archon-protected.yaml`.
82	
83	**Forbidden shortcuts (this objective specifically).**
84	- Do **not** close `genus` with a constant (e.g. `genus C := 0` or `:= 1`). Mathematically false on most curves; rejected on sight.
85	- Do **not** change the protected signature. Argument names, argument types, argument order, and return type `: ℕ` are frozen by `archon-protected.yaml`. The `noncomputable` modifier is already added by the iter-011 refactor; the prover only writes the body.
86	- Do **not** introduce new `axiom` declarations. The body is a single Mathlib + project-API application; no axioms are needed.
87	- Do **not** use `Sheaf.H` (the `AddCommGrpCat`-only Mathlib cohomology) in place of `Scheme.HModule`. The two have different unit objects (`AddCommGrpCat.of (ULift ℤ)` vs `ModuleCat.of k k`); the iter-010 plan-agent re-probe confirmed the comparison map is mathematically subtle and not a one-liner. Use `Scheme.HModule` directly.
88	- Do **not** rebuild `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` or `Scheme.HModule`. Both are pre-existing project declarations (iter-006 and iter-009 respectively); just consume them.
89	- Do **not** import additional Mathlib modules. The post-refactor `import Mathlib` + `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK` is sufficient.
90	- Do **not** write a `by` block, `exact`, `apply`, or any tactic. The body is a single term-mode application.
91	
92	**Compilation expectation.** After the body is filled, `lean_diagnostic_messages AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` should return `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` — zero warnings, zero errors. The `declaration uses 'sorry'` warning (currently at line 65 column 19) should disappear.
93	
94	**Axiom expectation.** `lean_verify` on `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` should return only the standard kernel axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. Any new axiom is forbidden.
95	
96	**Sorry-count expectation.** Pre-prover (post-refactor): 10 across 4 files. Post-prover: 9 across 3 files (the `Genus.lean` entry should fall to 0 sorries). Verify with `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary`.
97	
98	**Output protocol — write `task_results/Genus.lean.md`.** List for the single sorry: (a) the closure body adopted (must be `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` modulo optional `AlgebraicGeometry.` / `CategoryTheory.` prefix expansion, which both resolve under the file's `open` directives — either spelling is acceptable); (b) any name adjustments forced by the current Mathlib snapshot (none expected — but document if forced); (c) confirmation that no new `axiom` declarations were introduced (must be "no"); (d) full `lean_diagnostic_messages` output for `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` (expected: `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`); (e) full `lean_verify` axiom output for `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` (expected: only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`). Plus: propose the `\leanok` markers on the corresponding blueprint blocks (`def:genus` statement and proof in `chapters/Genus.tex`); the review agent will confirm and apply them.
99	
100	## Anticipated post-prover state
101	
102	- **Sorry count**: 10 → 9. Distribution: 5 `Jacobian.lean`, 3 `AbelJacobi.lean`, 0 `Genus.lean`, 1 `Picard/Functor.lean` (the deferred `representable` sorry remains).
103	- **Compilation**: `Genus.lean` returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`.
104	- **Axioms**: `lean_verify` on `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` returns kernel-only.
105	- **Blueprint**: `\leanok` markers on the statement and proof of `def:genus` (added by the iter-011 review agent).
106	- **First protected closure of the project.** `task_done.md` will gain its first protected-declaration entry; `task_pending.md`'s "Iter-011 plan" section will be migrated.
107	
108	## Off-limits this iteration
109	
110	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` (5 protected sorries) — gated on Phase C representability + Albanese, multi-iteration. The user's `noncomputable` authorisation extends here but is not actionable yet.
111	- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` (3 protected sorries) — gated on `Jacobian C` first, multi-iteration. The user's authorisation extends to `ofCurve` but is not actionable yet.
112	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` — `PicardFunctor.representable` (L185) is documented dead-end deferral until `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` lands in Mathlib + the FGA argument is built.
113	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean`, `Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean`, `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`, `Picard/LineBundle.lean`, `Picard/FunctorAb.lean`, `Rigidity.lean` — closed in earlier iterations; nothing to do this round.
114	
115	## Known dead ends (do not retry)
116	
117	- The 8 currently-blocked protected sorries (`Jacobian.lean` × 5, `AbelJacobi.lean` × 3) — gated on Phase C representability + Phase E geometric content + multi-iteration upstream Mathlib advances. Do not assign as direct prover objectives. The user's `noncomputable` authorisation removes the modifier-level gate but the upstream content gates remain.
118	- `PicardFunctor.representable` (`Picard/Functor.lean` L185) — closing on the global-sections-approximate `LineBundle` is a forbidden shortcut. Honest closure requires `LineBundle` refinement (gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules`, still absent in current Mathlib) plus the FGA argument. Multi-iteration; do not retry.
119	- `LineBundle` direct refinement — gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` (still absent; iter-011 re-probe). Multi-iteration; do not issue as a primary objective.
120	- Vacuous-functor closures (`Discrete PUnit`, etc.) — destroy the downstream Picard chain; rejected on sight.
121	- Vacuous-sheaf closures (`PUnit.{u+1}`-valued, etc.) — destroy the downstream cohomology chain; rejected on sight.
122	- For iter-011 `genus`: closing with anything other than `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` (or a mathematically-equivalent rebuild). The body is a single-application Mathlib closure on probe-confirmed identifiers; no creativity is required or expected.
123	- For iter-011 `genus`: closing with a constant (`genus C := 0`, `:= 1`, etc.) — false on most curves; mathematically vacuous; rejected on sight.
124	- For iter-011 `genus`: changing the protected signature beyond the `noncomputable` modifier addition (already done by the refactor agent). Argument types, names, and order are frozen.
125	- New `axiom` declarations — forbidden by plan-agent rules.
126	- Symmetric topological-equality form of rigidity (`∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x ⇒ g₁ = g₂`) — false in characteristic `p` (Frobenius). Resolved iter-002.
127	- Naive `AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor` (without explicit universe annotations) — does not unify under `u+1 =?= max u v`; explicit `.{u+1, u}` annotation required (resolved iter-008).
128	- `HModule` as `def` instead of `noncomputable abbrev` — instance synthesis fails on the wrapper (probe-verified iter-009).
129	- `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` with `≃` (`homEquiv₀`) or `≃+` (`addEquiv₀`) instead of `≃ₗ[k]` (`linearEquiv₀`) — the chosen signature is `≃ₗ[k]`; downstream `Module.Finite k` transport requires the `k`-linear refinement (resolved iter-010).
130	- Polish-shape `HModule_forget` (`forget₂ HModule ↔ Sheaf.H ((sheafCompose _ (forget₂)).obj F)`) — Mathlib's `Sheaf.H` uses `AddCommGrpCat.of (ULift ℤ)` as the unit, whereas `HModule` uses `ModuleCat.of k k`; the comparison is not a one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent finding, supersedes session-15 `recommendations.md`).
131	- Track 1 / Phase A step 6 *proper* (Serre finiteness on a proper curve) — multi-iteration; not in scope for iter-011. Path-2 Čech scaffold (Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor` is now available — useful background) deferred to iter-012+.

140	5. **Protected signature unchanged**: `archon-protected.yaml` unchanged; the `noncomputable def genus { … } : ℕ` signature line in `Genus.lean` shows only the `noncomputable` modifier added relative to iter-010 (already verified this pass for the refactor).
141	6. **Blueprint consistency**: `leanblueprint checkdecls` returns no errors; the `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genus}` reference in `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` resolves.

Now I'll read the prover prompt and blueprint for full context.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing

95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean

## AlgebraicGeometry.genus (line 65, body previously line 75 `sorry`)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Drop in the probe-confirmed one-liner from `PROGRESS.md` (Iter-011 plan).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — first edit, no adjustments forced.

### Closure record (per PROGRESS.md output protocol)

(a) **Closure body adopted (verbatim):**

```lean
Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
```

This matches the plan-agent's probe exactly (modulo no-prefix-expansion: under
the file's `namespace AlgebraicGeometry` + `open CategoryTheory Limits`, the
short names `Scheme.HModule` and `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` resolve to
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule` and `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf`
respectively). `Module.finrank` is the standard Mathlib spelling.

(b) **Name adjustments forced by current Mathlib snapshot:** None. The body
typechecks at universe `u` against the post-refactor imports
(`import Mathlib` + `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`)
on the first edit. The `Module k`-instance on
`Scheme.HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is auto-resolved through the
`noncomputable abbrev` reducibility of `HModule` (iter-009) plus
`Abelian.Ext.instModule`, exactly as the plan agent predicted.

(c) **No new `axiom` declarations introduced.** The closure is a single
term-mode application of pre-existing project + Mathlib API. Verified by
`lean_verify` axiom output below — only the standard kernel triple appears.

(d) **`lean_diagnostic_messages` output for `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean`:**

```json
{"success": true, "items": [], "failed_dependencies": []}
```

Zero errors, zero warnings. The previous `declaration uses 'sorry'` warning at
line 65 column 19 has cleared.

(e) **`lean_verify` axiom output for `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`:**

```json
{"axioms": ["propext", "Classical.choice", "Quot.sound"], "warnings": []}
```

Kernel-only — exactly the expected `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`
triple. No new axioms, no `sorryAx`.

### Sorry-count delta

`${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary`
returns:

```
Sorry Summary: 9 total across 3 file(s)
--------------------------------------------------
  AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean: 5 sorries
  AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean: 3 sorries
  AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean: 1 sorries
```

Pre-prover: 10 across 4 files. Post-prover: 9 across 3 files.
`AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` no longer appears — its single sorry is closed.
Matches the iter-011 expectation exactly.

### Protected signature preservation

The protected declaration `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` retains its frozen
signature verbatim:

```lean
noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ
```

Argument names, types, order, and return type `ℕ` are unchanged. Only the
body (formerly `sorry`) is now the closure expression. The `noncomputable`
modifier was added in the iter-011 refactor (user-authorised `2026-05-07`)
and is preserved.

### Forbidden shortcuts — none used

No constants, no signature changes, no new axioms, no `Sheaf.H` substitution,
no rebuild of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` / `Scheme.HModule`, no extra imports,
no tactic mode. Pure single-application term-mode closure as instructed.

### Blueprint markers proposal (for the review agent to apply)

`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex`:

- `\begin{definition}[Genus of a smooth proper curve] ... \end{definition}` at
  L10–19 (label `def:genus`): add `\leanok` — the Lean declaration
  `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` exists and compiles with the protected signature
  intact.
- `\begin{proof} ... \end{proof}` block at L21–34 (proof of `def:genus`): add
  `\leanok` — the proof body is fully closed with no `sorry`, kernel-only
  axioms, matches the informal closure expression in the proof block exactly.

### Status

**RESOLVED — first protected sorry of the project closed.** No follow-up
needed for this objective. The iter-011 prover round meets all four
PROGRESS.md verification criteria (sorry count, compilation, axioms, body
matches probe) on the first edit.
